test.details: 501 tests passed across 15 test files in 560ms

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: 'Filters must import stripAnsi() from src/utils.ts for ANSI stripping. Do not define local strip() helpers — the shared utility handles both SGR and OSC 8 sequences.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: All 11+ filter files (ls.ts, grep.ts, lint-tsc/js/py/rs.ts, test-js/py/rs/go.ts, git-status/diff.ts) define identical local strip() functions instead of using the shared stripAnsi() from src/utils.ts. This DRY violation was flagged by all 4 review subagents independently.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add note documenting the standard filter file structure: export a factory function (createXxxFilter) returning a Filter object with name, matches(cmd), and apply(cmd, raw) methods. Include parsing, formatting, and helper functions as module-private.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: All filter files follow this pattern consistently (ls-filter, test-runner-filters, lint-filters, grep-filter) but it's not codified. Documented in lint-filters and test-runner-filters reviews.

rejectedObservations.observation: NOISE_DIRS set should be shared across filters to prevent drift
rejectedObservations.reason: Currently only the ls filter uses noise directory filtering. No other filter has needed it yet. Premature to extract a shared constant — can be done if/when a second consumer appears.

rejectedObservations.observation: AGENTS.md should document the createXxxFilter factory function pattern for filters
rejectedObservations.reason: Promoted to suggestedGuidanceUpdates instead of rejected — this is a legitimate pattern worth codifying.
